segments.text: Picture a logo born in 1366, 660 years ago, and still on every Stella Arttoise
bottle today. Most brands tweak or redesign their logo every few years. But Stella Arttoise has kept its horn symbol
through wars, mergers, and even style revolutions for 660 years, outlasting centuries of trends. It started as the
horn tavern sign. When Sebastian Artoys bought the brewery in 1717, he kept the horn and later added Stella, which means
the star. Over the year, designers tried various styles like bold 1960 frames and detailed 1980s crests, but always returned to the original horn symbol.
That horn proves the power of timeless design. One single meaningful icon can survive for 6 and 1/2 centuries and
still feel instantly familiar. If you like this, follow for more such amazing design content.
